summary: Iteration 19 - Game Content Access Gating. All 7 features tested and verified. Backend: 8/8 tests passed. Frontend: Both games load real questions from database (not mock fallback). The /api/trivia/questions/for-game endpoint correctly gates access based on auth + purchase status.
critical_code_review_comments: trivia_game.py: /questions/for-game endpoint correctly implements entitlement check via _check_user_has_purchase. Demo users capped at 10 questions, purchasers/admins get full pool.
MixUpGame.js: fetchQuestions correctly calls /for-game with auth token from localStorage. Falls back to browse endpoint then mock questions if API fails.
TrickyTestamentGame.js: fetchQuestions correctly calls /for-game with auth token. Jeopardy board loads 10 questions from API response.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Game content access gating fully tested. The /api/trivia/questions/for-game endpoint returns access_level=demo (max 10 questions) for unauthenticated users and access_level=full for purchasers/admins. MixUp game uses tricky_trivia game_type, TrickyTestament uses trivia_testament game_type.

MixUp_game_loads_real_questions.details: Game at /game/mixup?edition=adult loads and shows real questions like 'Rahab acted out of...' and 'Boaz praised Ruth's...'

TrickyTestament_game_loads_real_question.details: Game at /game/tricky-testament?edition=adult loads Jeopardy board with 5 categories and real questions like 'What city did Rahab live in when she hid the Israelite spies?'
